title: Bytes, Encodings, and Cryptographic Primitive Selection · Execution Trace: Crypto Go Domain Separation
summary: An API team base64-encodes tokens, labels the result encrypted, hashes secrets for transport, and compares text representations without defining canonical bytes. Predict the asset, adversary, security property, trust boundary, and falsifying evidence before opening the governing standard. Planned artifact: Add and verify the domain separation increment in byte representation and primitive decision table. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugg…
objectives: Assign unambiguous labels, contexts, versions, roles, and byte encodings so one cryptographic use cannot be replayed as another.
Choose a vetted high-level construction that supplies the required combined properties instead of composing low-level primitives casually.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Cryptography and Go Predict Crypto Go Domain Separation · Theory Crypto Go Domain Separation
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory cryptographic engineering team handles case 9065 during a signed release review. Build deterministic Go evidence to separate encoding, hashing, authentication, confidentiality, reversibility, and canonical domain-bound bytes; cap bytes, work, memory, time, attempts, concurrency, and retained secret material before use; then change one authenticated byte and prove clean rejection without partial effect. Browser code uses pure state models or the narrow side-effect-free crypto allowlist with fixed fixtures. Production entropy and keys, secret custody, native timing and side channels, networks, TLS, HSM/KMS, certificate systems, FIPS or compliance state, load, faults, restore, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Assign unambiguous labels, contexts, versions, roles, and byte encodings so one cryptographic use cannot be replayed as another.
